title: Mediation, an Old Method with a New Twist
sub_topics: Advocates' Forum for Media (March 16, 2001, Linden Suites Ortigas)
negotiation / conciliation / mediation / arbitration as ADR methods
Rule 18 of the Rules of Court (pretrial ADR consideration)
Electronic Commerce Act and Retail Trade Liberalization Law (ADR encouragement)
Purefoods Corporation v. CA (GR 128066, June 19, 2000) — 1992 power-crisis generators settled in 2000
Annabelle T. Abaya as mediation trainer (UMass + Harvard)
300 trial courts in Metro Manila, Cebu, Davao
Senate impeachment trial vocabulary ('your Honor,' 'objection sustained,' 'irrelevant and immaterial')

stances.claim: In ancient days, sheer force decided conflicts — 'might dictated right' — but 'gentility and civilization have taught us that in the long term, right is better than might; the pen, more powerful than the sword; and reason, more reliable than aggression.'
stances.rhetorical_move: civilization-as-progress framing + chiasmic-doctrinal aphorism (recurring CJ right-vs-might theme)

stances.claim: Litigation has many disadvantages — 'tedious, time-consuming, stressful, expensive, and most often, ends up in broken relationships and permanent animosities'; judgments sometimes 'inflict deeper wounds and engender vengeance as well as new cycles of violence' instead of healing controversies.

Mediation conforms with the Asian and Filipino ways of solving problems 'without loss of face to the claimants' — being less confrontational, conducted tactfully and diplomatically, it can even improve relationships and interactions between parties after the problem is solved.
stances.rhetorical_move: cultural-affinity framing of ADR + face-preservation lens

stances.claim: Mediation is especially important in cases of continuing relationships — family disagreements, labor and tenancy problems (especially in special economic and freeport zones), commercial disputes, consumer controversies, and barangay-level disagreements.
stances.rhetorical_move: structured-domain enumeration for ADR's optimal application

stances.claim: ADRs are 'old methods with a new twist' — though some are older than litigation itself, recent refinement and polishing have made them more effective and acceptable for settling controversies; their promotion forms an integral part of the SC's judicial-reform program.
stances.rhetorical_move: title-phrase framing (old method / new twist) as institutional-narrative device

cases: Purefoods Corporation v. Court of Appeals (June 19, 2000)
laws_treaties: Rule 18 of the Rules of Court (pretrial)

signature_phrases: right is better than might; the pen, more powerful than the sword; and reason, more reliable than aggression
old methods with a new twist
without loss of face to the claimants
the Asian and Filipino ways of solving problems
register_markers: title-phrase repetition ('old method with a new twist')
decision_framework_signals: applies civilization-as-progress framing (might → right) for legal-system evolution
uses structured-disadvantages critique of litigation as ADR rationale
frames ADR-cultural-affinity (face-preservation, Asian/Filipino ways) as adoption-justification
applies continuing-relationships diagnostic to identify ADR-optimal case categories
cross_references: Chapters 1 and 2 of Justice Panganiban's earlier book, Transparency, Unanimity & Diversity (2000 ed., pp. 3-45)
notable_anecdotes: Purefoods v. CA: 1992 power-crisis generator dispute finally decided in 2000 — 'when the crisis was already over and the generators no longer needed by the buyer'
Senate impeachment trial making 'your Honor,' 'objection sustained,' and 'irrelevant and immaterial' part of ordinary public vocabulary
